hallucinations.description: The paper contains numerous placeholder citations (e.g., **??**, **?**, **??**) that do not correspond to any real academic papers. These are not valid references and appear to be hallucinated or left unresolved during generation.
hallucinations.evidence: Multiple instances throughout the paper, including in the Introduction ('**??**', '**?**'), Related Work ('**?**', '**??**'), and Discussion ('**?**').

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found, primarily in the form of nonexistent/placeholder citations. The experimental methodology and results appear consistent with the provided code (simulated user responses on HuggingFace datasets), with no clear evidence of faked results or mathematical errors. However, the citation issues are severe and render the paper invalid as a research document.
